data:
Composable page blocks for rapid application development.
Blocks are pre-wired layouts that combine multiple design system
components into production-ready page sections.
data.available-blocks#Available Blocks: Block	Description	PageHeader	Title, breadcrumbs, and action toolbar	PageBuilder	Grid + filters + toolbar composition	DetailPageLayout	Form + sidebar + tab navigation	DashboardGrid	Responsive widget grid layout	MetricStripBlock	KPI metric cards in a row	DataGridFilterChips	Composable filter chip bar	EmptyState	Illustrated empty state placeholder	ErrorBoundary	Error fallback with retry action	LoadingSkeleton	Shimmer loading placeholders	WizardLayout	Multi-step wizard container	SplitView	Resizable two-pane layout	CommandPalette	Keyboard-driven command search	NotificationCenter	Toast and notification stack	ConfirmDialog	Confirmation modal with variants

data:
Blocks are prebuilt UI sections that ship with sensible defaults but are designed
to be customized for your application's needs.
data.common-override-patterns#Common Override: Prop Pattern	Purpose	columns	Control which data columns are visible	pageSize	Set default pagination size	onRowClick	Handle row selection behavior	emptyState	Custom empty-state component	toolbar	Replace or extend the toolbar actions	filterConfig	Preset active filters
data.available-slots#Available Slots:
Each block documents its slots in its TypeScript interface. Common slots include:
Slot	Description	header	Top section with title and actions	toolbar	Action bar above the main content	content	Primary content area	sidebar	Optional side panel	footer	Bottom section with pagination or CTAs	
Pass null to any slot to remove that section. Pass a React element to replace
it entirely.
data.creating-custom-blocks#Creating Custom B:
When the built-in customization options are not enough, you can create a fully
custom block. See the Creating Blocks guide for the
full walkthrough.The recommended approach is to compose existing x-suite components and
design-system primitives into your own block, then register it in the block
registry so it appears in the Design Lab catalog.

data.runtime-support#Runtime Support: Environment	Supported Versions	Status	React	18.x, 19.x	Tested	Next.js	13.x, 14.x, 15.x	Tested	Vite	5.x, 6.x	Tested	Webpack	5.x	Tested	Node.js	20.x, 22.x	Tested	TypeScript	5.5+	Required
data.browser-support#Browser Support: Browser	Version	Status	Chrome	Last 2 major	Full support	Firefox	Last 2 major	Full support	Safari	16+	Full support	Edge	Last 2 major	Full support	Mobile Safari (iOS)	16+	Full support	Chrome Android	Last 2 major	Full support
data.peer-dependencies#Peer Dependencies: Package	Required Version	Notes	react	^18.0.0 || ^19.0.0	Peer dependency	react-dom	^18.0.0 || ^19.0.0	Peer dependency	ag-grid-community	^34.0.0	X-Data-Grid only	ag-grid-enterprise	^34.0.0	X-Data-Grid (server-side features)	ag-charts-community	^12.0.0	X-Charts only
data.module-formats#Module Formats:
ESM: Primary format, tree-shakeable
CJS: CommonJS for legacy Node.js tools
TypeScript: Full .d.ts declarations included

data.semver-commitment#Semver Commitment:
We follow Semantic Versioning 2.0:
Patch (1.0.x): Bug fixes, no API changes
Minor (1.x.0): New features, backward-compatible
Major (x.0.0): Breaking changes with migration path
data.deprecation-timeline#Deprecation Timelin:
Deprecation notice: Feature marked deprecated with console warning in dev mode
Migration guide: Published in docs with codemod when possible
Minimum 2 minor versions: Deprecated API remains functional
Removal: In next major version only
data.current-deprecations#Current Deprecation: Component	Deprecated API	Replacement	Since	Removal Target	Badge	tone prop	variant prop	v2.8	v3.0	Segmented	appearance prop	variant prop	v2.8	v3.0	Select	selectSize prop	size prop	v2.7	v3.0
data.codemod-support#Codemod Support:
Automated codemods are provided for most breaking changes:
npx @mfe/codemod@latest --transform badge-tone-to-variant ./src

data.installation#Installation:
Install the core design system package:
pnpm add @mfe/design-system
For X Suite packages, install individually:
pnpm add @mfe/x-data-grid @mfe/x-charts @mfe/x-editor
data.next-steps#Next Steps:
Explore the X Suite packages for enterprise features
Browse Blocks for pre-built page compositions
Check Migration guides if coming from another library

Check	Tool	Gate	Type safety	tsc --noEmit	PR merge block	Unit + integration	Vitest	PR merge block	Build output	tsup / Vite	PR merge block	Lint	ESLint + Prettier	PR merge block	Commit format	commitlint	PR merge block

data:
Enterprise-grade React component library with 100+ components,
6 X Suite packages, and a composable block system.
data.architecture#Architecture:
The design system follows a layered architecture:
Core — Theme tokens, utility hooks, base primitives
Components — 112 production-ready UI components
X Suite — Six domain-specific enterprise packages
Blocks — Composable page-level building blocks
All packages share a single theme provider and are tree-shakeable.
data.quick-links#Quick Links:
Architecture Overview
Getting Started with create-app
Component API Reference
Blocks Library
X Suite — Data Grid
X Suite — Charts
X Suite — Editor
X Suite — Form Builder
X Suite — Kanban
X Suite — Scheduler
Theme Customization
Troubleshooting
Performance SLA
Compatibility Matrix
First Contribution

data:
Data visualization package with chart components, dashboard widgets,
and composition hooks for enterprise dashboards.
data.quick-start#Quick Start:
import { BarChart, LineChart, PieChart } from '@mfe/x-charts';
<BarChart
  data={salesData}
  xField="month"
  yField="revenue"
  color="primary"
/>
data.chart-types#Chart Types: Component	Use Case	BarChart	Category comparisons	LineChart	Trends over time	AreaChart	Volume trends	PieChart	Part-to-whole ratios	ScatterChart	Correlation analysis	RadarChart	Multi-axis comparison	GaugeChart	KPI indicators	HeatmapChart	Density visualization	TreemapChart	Hierarchical proportions	WaterfallChart	Incremental value changes
data.dashboard-widgets#Dashboard Widgets: Component	Purpose	ChartDashboard	Grid layout for multiple charts	ChartContainer	Wrapper with loading/error states	SparklineChart	Inline mini sparkline	MiniChart	Compact chart for cards	KPICard	KPI metric display with trend	StatWidget	Single-stat summary widget	ChartLegend	Standalone legend component	ChartToolbar	Chart action toolbar (zoom, export)
data.theming#Theming:
Charts inherit the global palette from ThemeProvider automatically.
Override per-chart with the color or palette props:
<LineChart
  data={metrics}
  xField="date"
  yField="users"
  palette={['#1a56db', '#e74c3c', '#2ecc71']}
  animate
/>
data.hooks#Hooks: Hook	Purpose	useChartInteractions	Click, hover, and selection handlers	useChartResize	Responsive container resize observer	useRealTimeData	Live-updating data stream adapter	useChartVariants	Chart variant configuration	useDashboardComposition	Cross-package: dashboard with shared date range	useResponsiveBreakpoint	Breakpoint detection for responsive charts	useResponsiveChartConfig	Auto-adjust chart config per breakpoint
data.responsive-behavior#Responsive Behavior:
All charts are responsive by default. Use the aspectRatio prop
to control proportions:
<BarChart data={data} xField="q" yField="sales" aspectRatio={16 / 9} />

data.issue-priority-levels#Issue Priority Lev: Priority	Definition	Response Target	Resolution Target	P0 — Critical	Production down, data loss	4 hours	24 hours	P1 — High	Major feature broken, no workaround	1 business day	3 business days	P2 — Medium	Feature degraded, workaround exists	3 business days	Next sprint	P3 — Low	Minor issue, cosmetic	1 week	Backlog
data.issue-template#Issue Template:
When reporting an issue, include:
Component name and version
Environment (React version, browser, OS)
Minimal reproduction steps
Expected vs actual behavior
Screenshots if visual
data.release-channels#Release Channels: Channel	Cadence	Stability	latest	Every 2-4 weeks	Production-ready	beta	Weekly	Feature-complete, minor bugs possible	canary	Daily	Unstable, for early testing
data.known-issues#Known Issues: Track known issues and workarounds at Known Issues.
data.contact#Contact:
Bug reports: GitHub Issues
Feature requests: GitHub Discussions
Security: security@example.com (see SECURITY.md)

data:
How to migrate from popular libraries to @mfe/design-system
and X Suite packages.
data.available-guides#Available Guides: From	To	Effort	MUI DataGrid	@mfe/x-data-grid	Medium	AG Grid (raw)	@mfe/x-data-grid	Low	Recharts	@mfe/x-charts	Low	Draft.js	@mfe/x-editor	High	react-beautiful-dnd	@mfe/x-kanban	Medium	FullCalendar	@mfe/x-scheduler	Medium	react-jsonschema-form	@mfe/x-form-builder	Low
data.general-approach#General Approach: Each migration follows a three-phase approach:
data.phase-2--incremental-replacement#Phase 2:
Replace adapters with direct @mfe usage one page at a time.
Run both libraries in parallel during the transition.
data.phase-3--cleanup#Phase 3 — Cleanup:
Remove the legacy library dependency and adapter code.
Run the full test suite to verify feature parity.
data.codemods#Codemods:
Automated codemods are available for common transformations:
npx @mfe/codemod mui-datagrid-to-xdg ./src
npx @mfe/codemod recharts-to-xcharts ./src
data.need-help#Need Help?:
Open an issue on the platform-ssot
repository with the migration label.

data: Quantifying the return on investment of a unified platform versus integrating 6 separate libraries.
data.unified-api-vs-6-separate-libraries#Unif: Activity	Unified Platform	6 Separate Libraries	Savings	Initial setup	1 install, 1 config	6 installs, 6 configs, compatibility checks	~4 hours per project	Learning curve	1 API pattern to learn	6 different API conventions	~40 hours per new developer	Component integration	Components share types and events natively	Custom adapters and type bridges between libraries	~8 hours per integration point	Debugging	Single source map, unified error handling	Context switching between 6 different debugging approaches	~30% faster issue resolution
data.estimated-annual-savings-10-person-team#: Category	Hours Saved / Year	Onboarding (2 new devs/year)	80 hours	Integration work	120 hours	Debugging efficiency	200 hours	Documentation lookup	100 hours	Total	~500 hours/year
data.single-token-system-vs-manual-alignment#: Dimension	Unified Platform	6 Separate Libraries	Theme changes	Update tokens once, all components update	Update 6 theme configs, verify each integration	Dark mode	One implementation, guaranteed consistency	6 separate dark mode implementations to maintain	Spacing / Typography	Enforced by shared tokens	Manual alignment, drift over time	Brand updates	Single PR, single review	6 PRs across 6 library configs
data.consistency-cost-without-unified-tokens#:
Designer time to audit cross-library consistency: ~4 hours/month
Developer time to fix visual mismatches: ~8 hours/month
Annual cost of inconsistency: ~144 hours/year
data.one-upgrade-path-vs-6-separate-update-cy: Activity	Unified Platform	6 Separate Libraries	Dependency updates	1 upgrade per release cycle	6 independent upgrade schedules	Breaking changes	1 migration guide	6 migration guides, potential cross-library conflicts	Security patches	1 audit, 1 patch cycle	6 separate vulnerability tracking processes	Version pinning	1 lockfile entry set	6 libraries with transitive dependency conflicts
data.estimated-annual-maintenance-overhead#Es: Task	Unified (hours/year)	6 Libraries (hours/year)	Delta	Major version upgrades	24	96	72	Security patching	12	48	36	Dependency conflict resolution	4	40	36	Regression testing after upgrades	16	64	48	Total	56	248	192 hours saved
data.single-ci-pipeline-vs-fragmented-testing: Dimension	Unified Platform	6 Separate Libraries	Test coverage	All components tested together in one suite	Each library tested in isolation; integration gaps	Visual regression	One Chromatic/Storybook pipeline	6 separate visual test setups (if they exist)	A11y testing	Single axe-core run covers everything	6 separate audit processes, inconsistent coverage	E2E confidence	Components guaranteed compatible	Cross-library interactions untested until production
data.quality-cost-without-unified-pipeline#Qu:
Time to set up and maintain 6 CI pipelines: ~60 hours/year
Production bugs from integration gaps: ~3 incidents/year at ~16 hours each = 48 hours
Visual inconsistency bug reports: ~24 hours/year
Annual quality cost: ~132 hours/year
data.total-roi-summary#Total ROI Summary:
Category	Annual Hours Saved	Developer time savings	500	Design consistency	144	Maintenance reduction	192	Quality improvement	132	Total	~968 hours/year	
At an average fully-loaded developer cost, this translates to significant annual savings while simultaneously improving product quality and developer satisfaction.
data.intangible-benefits#Intangible Benefits:
Beyond measurable time savings:
Faster onboarding: New team members become productive sooner with one API to learn.
Higher developer satisfaction: Less time fighting integration issues, more time building features.
Predictable release cadence: Stakeholders can plan around a single release schedule.
Reduced decision fatigue: No recurring debates about which library to use for new features.
Consistent user experience: End users get a cohesive product, not a patchwork of different UI paradigms.

data:
Recipe layer over AG Grid 34.3 with column builder,
server-side patterns, and cross-package composition.
data.core-components#Core Components: Export	Description	EntityGridTemplate	Primary grid component (re-exported from design-system)	GridShell	Low-level grid shell wrapper	GridToolbar	Toolbar for grid actions	TablePagination	Pagination control
data.recipe-components#Recipe Components: Export	Description	DataGridFilterChips	Active filter chip display	DataGridSelectionBar	Bulk-selection action bar	ServerDataSource	Server-side data source adapter	MasterDetailGrid	Expandable rows with nested grids	TreeDataGrid	Hierarchical row grouping	PivotGrid	Pivot table layout	EditableGrid	Inline cell editing	RowGroupingGrid	Row grouping with aggregation
data.column-builder#Column Builder:
The fluent useColumnBuilder API provides type-safe column definitions:
const columns = useColumnBuilder<Product>()
  .text('name', { flex: 2 })
  .number('stock', { editable: true })
  .currency('price', { currency: 'TRY' })
  .action('edit', { icon: 'pencil', onClick: handleEdit })
  .build();
data.hooks#Hooks: Hook	Purpose	useColumnBuilder	Fluent column definition API	useGridExport	Excel/CSV export (ExcelExportOptions, CsvExportOptions)	useGridState	Grid state persistence (column order, sort, filters)	useGridChartDrilldown	Cross-package: grid-to-chart drill-down	useGridFormSync	Cross-package: grid-to-form synchronization

data:
Rich text editing package with Tiptap-based components for content
authoring, slash commands, mentions, and collaborative editing patterns.
data.components#Components: Component	Purpose	RichTextEditor	Full-featured WYSIWYG editor	TiptapEditor	Direct Tiptap integration editor	EditorToolbar	Configurable toolbar strip	EditorMenuBubble	Floating selection menu	SlashCommandMenu	Slash-command block insertion menu	MentionList	@mention autocomplete dropdown	EditorTableMenu	Table manipulation controls	EditorLinkDialog	URL insertion with preview	EditorImageUpload	Drag-and-drop image insertion
data.toolbar-customization#Toolbar Customizat:
Build custom toolbars by composing toolbar items:
<RichTextEditor
  toolbar={[
    'bold', 'italic', 'underline',
    '|',
    'heading', 'bulletList', 'orderedList',
    '|',
    'link', 'image', 'table',
  ]}
/>
data.hooks#Hooks: Hook	Purpose	useEditor	Core editor state and commands	useTiptapEditor	Tiptap-specific editor binding	useSlashCommands	Slash command registration and handling	useMentions	Mention autocomplete logic	useEditorFormField	Cross-package: editor as a form field

data: An honest assessment of how our unified platform compares to assembling individual best-of-breed libraries.
data.component-coverage#Component Coverage: Category	Our Platform	Alternative	Data Grid	Built-in (AG Grid powered)	AG Grid / MUI X Data Grid	Charts	Built-in	Recharts / Nivo / MUI X Charts	Rich Text Editor	Built-in	Tiptap / Lexical / Slate	Scheduler / Calendar	Built-in	FullCalendar / MUI X Date Pickers	Kanban Board	Built-in	react-beautiful-dnd + custom	Form Builder	Built-in	SurveyJS / React Hook Form + custom
data.dimension-comparison#Dimension Compariso: Dimension	Our Platform	Mix of 6 Libraries	Bundle Size	Single tree-shakeable bundle, shared dependencies deduplicated	Each library ships its own runtime; duplicated utilities and styles	TypeScript	Strict mode across all components, unified type definitions	Varies per library; some have incomplete or @types/ packages	Accessibility (A11y)	Consistent WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, single audit process	Each library has its own a11y posture; gaps between integrations	Theme Integration	Single design token system, one theme applied everywhere	Manual alignment between 6 different theming approaches	SSR Support	Verified across all components	Mixed support; some libraries require workarounds	Enterprise Support	Single vendor, single SLA, single point of contact	6 separate license agreements, support channels, and upgrade paths
data.integration-complexity#Integration Compl: Concern	Our Platform	Mix of 6 Libraries	API Consistency	Unified prop patterns, shared event model, common data types	Each library defines its own API conventions	Styling	One token system, one CSS strategy	Competing CSS approaches (CSS-in-JS, CSS modules, inline styles)	State Management	Components work with a single store pattern	Each library may impose its own state model	Testing	Single test harness, unified mocking utilities	Different testing approaches per library	Upgrades	One release cadence, one changelog, one migration guide	6 independent release schedules with potential breaking changes
data.where-alternatives-excel#Where Alternati:
We believe in honest comparison. Here is where individual libraries may have an edge:
Library	Strength	AG Grid	Deepest feature set for extreme data grid use cases (100k+ rows, server-side row model, pivoting)	MUI X	Largest community and ecosystem, extensive documentation	Tiptap	Most extensible editor architecture with collaborative editing	FullCalendar	Widest calendar view options and plugin ecosystem	SurveyJS	Purpose-built survey engine with advanced logic branching
data.our-advantage#Our Advantage:
The value of a unified platform is not in any single component being the absolute best in its category. The value is in the compound effect of everything working together:
Single design system: Every component shares the same visual language, spacing scale, color tokens, and interaction patterns.
Single release cadence: One upgrade, one changelog, one migration guide. No dependency matrix to manage across 6 libraries.
Single CI pipeline: All components tested together. Breaking changes caught before they reach production.
Unified developer experience: Learn one API pattern, apply it everywhere. No context switching between library conventions.
Guaranteed compatibility: Grid, charts, editor, scheduler, kanban, and forms are tested together in every release.
data.decision-framework#Decision Framework:
Choose our platform when:
You need 3+ component categories from the table above.
Design consistency across the application is a priority.
You want a single upgrade and support path.
Your team values unified developer experience over per-component depth.
Consider individual libraries when:
You only need one component category.
You need a feature that is unique to a specific library (e.g., collaborative editing via Tiptap).
You have an existing investment in a specific library ecosystem.

data: Current known issues, limitations, and planned resolutions for the @mfe platform.
data.ag-grid-v34-enterprise-features-require-:
Severity: Expected behavior
Impact: Server-Side Row Model (SSRM), integrated charts, and Excel export are unavailable without an AG Grid Enterprise license key.Affected packages: @mfe/x-data-gridWorkaround: Use client-side row model for datasets under 50K rows. Use CSV export instead of Excel export. Use @mfe/x-charts as a standalone chart solution instead of AG Grid integrated charts.Planned fix: License provisioning documented in deployment guide. Enterprise license is included in production builds via AG_GRID_LICENSE_KEY environment variable.
data.vite-cjs-deprecation-warnings#Vite CJS D:
Severity: Cosmetic (no functional impact)
Impact: Console warnings during vite dev and vite build:
The CJS build of Vite's Node API is deprecated. See https://vite.dev/guide/troubleshooting.html
Affected packages: All packages using Vite for dev/buildWorkaround: Warnings can be suppressed with NODE_NO_WARNINGS=1 but this is not recommended as it hides other useful warnings. The warnings have no impact on build output or runtime behavior.Planned fix: Full ESM migration tracked for v1.6. Requires all config files to use .mts extension and "type": "module" in package.json.
data.react-19-support#React 19 Support:
Severity: Planned limitation
Impact: React 19 is not officially supported in v1.x. While basic rendering may work, the following are known issues:
forwardRef deprecation warnings (React 19 makes ref a regular prop)
Suspense boundary behavior changes may affect lazy-loaded remotes
New use() hook not yet leveraged
Affected packages: All packagesWorkaround: Stay on React 18.x for production. React 19 can be tested with the next release channel.Planned fix: React 19 support is the headline feature of @mfe v2.0, scheduled for Q4 2026. Migration guide will be published 3 months before release.
data.safari--15-not-supported#Safari < 15 Not:
Severity: Platform limitation
Impact: The following features do not work in Safari versions prior to 15:
CSS gap in flexbox layouts (Safari 14.1+)
dialog element (Safari 15.4+)
CSS color-mix() for theme tokens (Safari 16.2+)
structuredClone used in state management (Safari 15.4+)
Affected packages: All packagesWorkaround: No polyfills are provided. Users on older Safari versions should update their browser or OS.Planned fix: This is a permanent platform boundary. The compatibility matrix documents all supported browser versions. Safari 15+ represents >97% of active Safari users as of early 2026.
data.tracking#Tracking: Issues linked to known limitations are tagged with the known-issue label in the repository. When a fix ships, the corresponding entry is removed from this page and a note is added to the changelog.

data:
This guide covers how to build a new block from scratch, test it, and register
it in the block registry.
data.block-anatomy#Block Anatomy:
A block is a self-contained UI section composed of design-system components and
x-suite widgets. Every block consists of two parts:
Component -- the React component that renders the block
Registry entry -- metadata that describes the block for the catalog
data.file-structure#File Structure:
packages/blocks/src/
  blocks/
    invoice-list/
      InvoiceListBlock.tsx        # Component
      InvoiceListBlock.test.tsx   # Tests
      InvoiceListBlock.stories.tsx # Storybook story
      index.ts                    # Re-export
  registry.ts                     # Block registry
  index.ts                        # Package entry
data.data-requirements#Data Requirements:
Blocks are data-agnostic by default. They accept data via props or fetch it
through a data hook. Follow these patterns:
data.props-driven-simple#Props-Driven (Simple:
The consumer fetches data and passes it in:
<InvoiceListBlock rows={invoices} loading={isLoading} />
data.run-tests#Run Tests:
# Unit tests for blocks package
pnpm --filter @mfe/blocks test
# Storybook visual check
pnpm storybook
data.registry-fields#Registry Fields: Field	Required	Description	component	Yes	The React component	meta.name	Yes	Human-readable name for the catalog	meta.description	Yes	One-line summary	meta.category	Yes	Grouping key (finance, crm, common, etc.)	meta.tags	No	Search keywords	meta.slots	No	List of available slot names	meta.dataContract	No	TypeScript type name for expected data shape
data.checklist#Checklist:
Before submitting your block PR, verify:
Component renders correctly with default props
All slots work (override, remove via null, and default)
TypeScript interface exported and documented with JSDoc
Unit tests cover default, empty, and slot-override scenarios
Storybook story exists with at least Default and Empty variants
Registry entry added with complete metadata
Re-exported from packages/blocks/src/index.ts

data:
Six specialized packages for enterprise application development.
Each package extends the core design system with domain-specific
components and patterns.
data.packages#Packages: Package	Purpose	Components	x-data-grid	AG Grid recipe layer	11	x-charts	Data visualization	18	x-editor	Rich text editing	13	x-kanban	Board & workflow views	11	x-scheduler	Calendar & scheduling	14	x-form-builder	Dynamic form generation	12
data.installation#Installation:
Install any X Suite package alongside the core library:
pnpm add @mfe/design-system @mfe/x-data-grid

data:
Board and workflow visualization with drag-and-drop columns,
swimlanes, and WIP limits.
data.components#Components: Component	Purpose	KanbanBoard	Full board with columns and drag-and-drop	KanbanColumn	Single column container	KanbanCard	Draggable card item	KanbanSwimlane	Horizontal row grouping	KanbanToolbar	Board-level action toolbar	KanbanCardDetail	Expanded card detail view	KanbanMetrics	Board metrics and analytics display
data.hooks#Hooks: Hook	Purpose	useKanban	Board state management	useDragDrop	Drag-and-drop engine (framework-agnostic)	useDndKitKanban	dnd-kit integration for kanban	useSortableCard	dnd-kit sortable card binding	useDroppableColumn	dnd-kit droppable column binding	useKanbanFilter	Board filtering logic	useWipPolicy	WIP limit enforcement and violation detection	useKanbanVariants	Board variant configuration	useKanbanSchedulerSync	Cross-package: kanban-to-scheduler sync	useResponsiveKanban	Mobile-responsive board layout
data.drag-and-drop#Drag and Drop:
Cards support drag-and-drop between columns with animated transitions.
The onMove callback receives source and destination metadata.
Use useDndKitKanban for the dnd-kit powered engine, or useDragDrop
for the framework-agnostic fallback.

data:
Calendar and scheduling package with view components for
event management, resource booking, and timeline views.
data.components#Components: Component	Purpose	Scheduler	Full calendar with view switching	DayView	Single day hour grid	WeekView	7-day columnar layout	MonthView	Monthly calendar grid	AgendaView	List-based event display	ResourceView	Resource allocation view	SchedulerEventCard	Draggable event block	SchedulerToolbar	Navigation and view controls	EventForm	Event create/edit form	CurrentTimeIndicator	Current time line overlay	MiniMonthNav	Compact month navigation sidebar
data.views#Views:
Switch between views with the view prop:
<Scheduler
  events={events}
  view="month"        // 'day' | 'week' | 'month' | 'agenda'
  resources={rooms}   // Required for resource view
/>
data.hooks#Hooks: Hook	Purpose	useScheduler	Core scheduler state and navigation	useRecurrence	Recurrence rule expansion	useConflictDetection	Overlapping event detection	useTimezone	Timezone conversion utilities	useEventDrag	Drag-to-reschedule event handling	useExternalDrop	External item drop onto calendar	useSchedulerVariants	View variant configuration	useResponsiveScheduler	Mobile-responsive scheduler layout

data: Common issues and their solutions when working with the monorepo.
data.1-ag-grid-license-warning#1. AG Grid Lic:
Symptom: Console warning about AG Grid enterprise license missing.Solution: Set the AG_GRID_LICENSE_KEY environment variable in your .env.local:
AG_GRID_LICENSE_KEY=your-license-key-here
The license is injected at build time via LicenseManager.setLicenseKey() in the
design-system grid wrapper. In CI, the key is stored as a repository secret.
Symptom: Build fails with Container reference "mfe_xxx" is not declared in externals.Solution:
Verify the remote name matches exactly between host and remote webpack configs.
Ensure the MFE dev server is running if you are in local development mode.
Check modulefederation.config.ts for typos in the remotes map.
# Start the shell + the MFE you need
pnpm dev:shell
pnpm --filter @mfe/crm dev
data.3-pnpm-workspace-resolution-issues#3. pn:
Symptom: ERR_PNPM_PEER_DEP_ISSUES or packages resolve to the wrong version.Solution:
# Clean and reinstall
rm -rf node_modules packages/*/node_modules
pnpm install
If a specific package version is pinned incorrectly, check pnpm-workspace.yaml
and the root package.json pnpm.overrides field.
data.5-mfedesign-system-module-not-found#5. @:
Symptom: Cannot find module '@mfe/design-system' at build or runtime.Solution:
Build the design-system first:
pnpm build:design-system
If using path aliases, verify tsconfig.json paths include:
{ "@mfe/design-system": ["packages/design-system/src/index.ts"] }
For Module Federation, confirm design-system is listed in shared config.
data.6-vite-cjs-deprecation-warning#6. Vite C:
Symptom: The CJS build of Vite's Node API is deprecated warning during dev.Solution: Ensure your config files use ESM syntax. Rename vite.config.js to
vite.config.ts or add "type": "module" to the package's package.json.This is a non-blocking warning and does not affect functionality in current versions.
data.8-design-system-dist-not-built#8. design:
Symptom: Consuming packages see stale or missing exports from @mfe/design-system.Solution:
pnpm build:design-system
During development, run the design-system in watch mode so changes propagate
immediately:
pnpm --filter @mfe/design-system dev
data.9-port-conflicts-3000-3001-etc#9. Port C:
Symptom: EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::3000.Solution:
# Find what is using the port
lsof -i :3000
# Kill it
kill -9 <PID>
Default port assignments:
Service	Port	Shell	3000	Design Lab	3001	Docs	3002	MFE remotes	3010+	
Override with PORT=XXXX environment variable.
data.10-typescript-strict-mode-errors#10. Typ:
Symptom: New TS2322 or TS7053 errors after enabling strict: true.Solution:
Fix implicit any types by adding explicit annotations.
For index signatures, use Record<string, T> or add an index signature.
The repo uses strict: true at the root. Per-package overrides in
tsconfig.json can relax rules temporarily during migration:
{ "compilerOptions": { "noImplicitAny": false } }
data.11-storybook-build-failures#11. Storyboo:
Symptom: ERR! Error: Failed to build the preview during pnpm build-storybook.Solution:
Clear the Storybook cache:
rm -rf node_modules/.cache/storybook
Ensure all peer dependencies are installed (especially @storybook/addon-*).
Verify .storybook/main.ts stories globs match actual file locations.
Check for circular imports -- Storybook's webpack build is less forgiving.
data.12-chromatic-baseline-mismatch#12. Chrom:
Symptom: Chromatic flags visual diffs that look identical on your screen.Solution:
Anti-aliasing and font rendering differ between your machine and Chromatic's
capture environment (Chrome on Linux).
Use chromatic: { diffThreshold: 0.2 } in story parameters for known
rendering-sensitive components.
For animations, disable them in the Storybook test decorator or set
chromatic: { delay: 500 } to wait for animations to settle.
data.13-hot-reload-not-working#13. Hot Reload:
Symptom: Code changes are saved but the browser does not update.Solution:
Check the terminal for HMR errors or disconnection messages.
Ensure you are editing files inside the src/ directory (files outside the
watched scope are ignored).
For Module Federation remotes, HMR only works within the remote's own code.
Changes to shared modules require a page reload.
On macOS, if FSEvents is broken:
brew install watchman
data.14-memory-issues-with-large-test-suites#:
Symptom: FATAL ERROR: CALL_AND_RETRY_LAST Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory.Solution:
# Increase Node heap size
NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" pnpm test
For CI, this is set in the workflow environment. Locally, add it to your shell
profile or .env.local.Also consider running tests in smaller batches:
pnpm --filter @mfe/design-system test
data.15-node-version-mismatch#15. Node Versio:
Symptom: error @mfe/design-system@1.0.0: The engine "node" is incompatible.Solution:The repo requires Node 22. Use a version manager:
# nvm
nvm install 22
nvm use 22
# fnm
fnm use 22
# Verify
node -v  # should print v22.x.x
The required version is specified in package.json under engines and in
.nvmrc at the repo root.
data.still-stuck#Still Stuck?:
Search existing issues in the repository.
Ask in the #frontend-platform channel.
Open an issue with the bug label and include reproduction steps.

data:
Schema-driven form renderer with JSON-schema-based runtime,
extensible field registry, and validation adapters.
Visual form designer (FormBuilder) is planned for v2.
data.core-components#Core Components: Component	Purpose	FormRenderer	Renders forms from a FormSchema	FormPreview	Live form preview pane	FieldRenderer	Single field renderer (with 12 built-in field types)	FormSectionComponent	Grouped field section	MultiStepForm	Multi-step wizard form	FormSummary	Review summary before submission	RepeatableFieldGroup	Dynamic array of field groups
data.built-in-field-renderers#Built-in Field:
TextFieldRenderer, NumberFieldRenderer, SelectFieldRenderer,
MultiselectFieldRenderer, CheckboxFieldRenderer, RadioFieldRenderer,
SwitchFieldRenderer, TextareaFieldRenderer, DateFieldRenderer,
TimeFieldRenderer, FileFieldRenderer, CustomFieldRenderer
data.hooks#Hooks: Hook	Purpose	useFormSchema	Form state from a FormSchema	useZodForm	Zod-powered form validation	useConditionalLogic	Evaluate conditional visibility rules	useAsyncValidation	Async field validation	useMultiStepForm	Multi-step wizard navigation and state
data.validation#Validation:
Built-in validators include required, min, max, pattern,
email, url, and custom async validators.Zod integration is available via createZodValidator, toZodSchema,
and fromZodSchema utilities.
